b:T7010,<div class="specification">
<p>A representation of the unit cell of gold is shown.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the name of the crystal structure of gold.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Calculate the number of atoms per unit cell of gold, showing your working.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The edge length of the gold unit cell is 4.08 × 10<sup>‒8</sup> cm.</p>
<p>Determine the density of gold in g cm<sup>‒3</sup>, using sections 2 and 6 of the data booklet.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>c:Tb9c,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>face-centred cube/fcc</p>
<p><em><strong>OR</strong></em></p>
<p>cubic close packed/ccp ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{1}{2}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mn>1</mn>
<mn>2</mn>
</mfrac>
</math></span> «atom per face» × 6 «faces per cube» × 3 «atoms» <em><strong>AND </strong></em><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{1}{8}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mn>1</mn>
<mn>8</mn>
</mfrac>
</math></span> «atom per corner» × 8 «corners per cube» = 1 «atom» ✔</p>
<p>«atoms per unit cell = 3 + 1 =» 4 ✔</p>
<p> </p>
<p><em>Award <strong>[1 max]</strong> for “4” without working shown.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>«4 atoms per unit cell»</p>
<p>mass of 4 atoms «<span class="mjpage"><math alttext=" = 4 \times \frac{{196.97\,{\text{g}}\,{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - 1}}}}{{6.02 \times {{10}^{23}}\,{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - 1}}}} = " xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>4</mn>
<mo>×</mo>
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>196.97</mn>
<mspace width="thinmathspace"></mspace>
<mrow>
<mtext>g</mtext>
</mrow>
<mspace width="thinmathspace"></mspace>
<mrow>
<mtext>mo</mtext>
</mrow>
<mrow>

<mrow>
<mtext>l</mtext>
</mrow>

<mrow>
<msup>
<mrow>
<mn>10</mn>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mn>23</mn>
</mrow>
</msup>
</mrow>
<mspace width="thinmathspace"></mspace>
<mrow>
<mtext>mo</mtext>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<msup>
<mrow>
<mtext>l</mtext>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mo>−</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
</mrow>
</msup>
</mrow>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
<mo>=</mo>
</math></span>» 1.31 × 10<sup>–21</sup> «g»</p>
<p>volume of unit cell «= (4.08 × 10<sup>‒8</sup>)<sup>3</sup> cm<sup>3</sup>» = 6.79 × 10<sup>–23</sup> «cm<sup>3</sup>»</p>
<p>density = «<span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{1.31 \times {{10}^{ - 21}}\,{\text{g}}}}{{6.79 \times {{10}^{ - 23}}\,{\text{c}}{{\text{m}}^3}}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>1.31</mn>
<mo>×</mo>

<msup>

<mn>10</mn>

<mo>−</mo>
<mn>21</mn>
</mrow>
</msup>
</mrow>
<mspace width="thinmathspace"></mspace>
<mrow>
<mtext>g</mtext>
</mrow>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mn>6.79</mn>
<mo>×</mo>
<mrow>

<mn>10</mn>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mo>−</mo>

</mrow>

<mspace width="thinmathspace"></mspace>
<mrow>

</mrow>
<mrow>
<msup>

</mrow>
<mn>3</mn>
</msup>
</mrow>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
</math></span>» = 1.93 × 10<sup>1</sup>/19.3 «g cm<sup>‒3</sup>»</p>
<p> </p>
<p><em>Award<strong> [3]</strong> for correct final answer.</em></p>
<p> </p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
